summary: P1 second-pass route cleanup verified end-to-end via Playwright with authenticated test session. All 5 legacy dashboard route redirects work correctly (client-side <Navigate>), Overview page 'Open Content Studio' quick-action now navigates to /dashboard/compose, Command Palette contains no forbidden entries (AI Team / Agent Chatter / Content Studio / Activity feed) and still contains 'Growth Team roster' and 'Compose & Publish'. Marketing home renders fine. One regression introduced by the cleanup: React duplicate-key warning on /dashboard/overview because two quickActions items now share the same `to='/dashboard/compose'` while the map uses `key={a.to}`.

ui_bugs.issue: React console warning: `Encountered two children with the same key, /dashboard/compose`. The 'Open Content Studio' tile (line 82) was repointed to /dashboard/compose in this cleanup, but the 'Compose & publish' tile (line 85) already uses that same href. Because the list renders with `key={a.to}` (line ~229), React now sees duplicate keys and may duplicate/omit tiles across updates. Reproducible on every visit to /dashboard/overview.

action_items: In /app/frontend/src/pages/dashboard/Overview.jsx, change the quickActions map key from `key={a.to}` to a unique value (e.g., `key={a.title}` or `key={`${a.title}-${a.to}`}`) OR drop the duplicate 'Compose & publish' tile / repoint one of them, since both tiles now navigate to /dashboard/compose. Fixes the React duplicate-key console warning introduced by this P1 cleanup.
critical_code_review_comments: App.js has 5 redirect routes that are effectively no-ops for internal links but essential for external/bookmarked traffic — good.
CommandPalette.jsx: icon imports (Activity, Wand2, MessagesSquare) confirmed pruned; no dead imports remain. lucide-react tree-shakes so bundle impact is negligible either way.

Overview.jsx quickActions currently has two visually distinct tiles that both link to /dashboard/compose ('Open Content Studio' and 'Compose & publish'). Consider consolidating or repointing one — either drop 'Open Content Studio' or point it to something more specific (e.g., /dashboard/compose?mode=studio). Otherwise users see two seemingly different CTAs that land on the same page.

success_rate.frontend: 100% for routing/redirect flow (5/5 redirects PASS, Overview link fixed, CommandPalette pruned correctly). 1 medium React warning regression.

test_credentials: Used test_session_1779636592168 (user_test1779636592168 / test@automatex.dev) from /app/memory/test_credentials.md via session_token cookie on the preview domain — /api/auth/me returned 200 confirming auth.
context_for_next_testing_agent: All redirect targets are correct and client-side (react-router <Navigate to=... replace/>). The `session_token` cookie approach with the preview host works — no need to run the Emergent Google auth flow for redirect tests. Playwright test scaffolding: set cookie, hit /api/auth/me for sanity, then goto(legacy_path) and read page.url after ~1.5s. Command Palette opens via `page.keyboard.press('Control+k')` and its items expose `data-testid=cmd-<path>` for reliable enumeration. If the main agent fixes the Overview.jsx duplicate-key warning, a simple re-run of /dashboard/overview and inspection of console errors is sufficient — no full regression required.
rca of the issue: Root cause: /app/frontend/src/pages/dashboard/Overview.jsx line 82 was repointed from `to: '/dashboard/studio'` to `to: '/dashboard/compose'` during the studio→compose cleanup. The tile at line 85 already had `to: '/dashboard/compose'`. The subsequent map uses `key={a.to}` when rendering tiles, so React now receives two children with the identical key `/dashboard/compose`, producing the console warning: `Encountered two children with the same key, %s`. Reproduction: log in, visit /dashboard/overview, open DevTools console — warning fires on mount. Mitigation: (a) change the map key to `key={a.title}` (unique), or (b) delete/repoint one of the two duplicate tiles. Option (a) is the minimal fix; option (b) also removes the UX oddity of two Quick-Action tiles that navigate to the same route.
